subject: Mathematics
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: VARIATION
topics: Overview: Variation in Mathematics describes the relationship between two quantities and how a
change in one quantity affects the other. It helps learners understand how variables are connected
and how mathematical models can represent real-life situations. In this topic, learners explore
different types of relationships between variables such direct, inverse, joint, and partial variations.
activities: age and height, water temperature and depth and so on
In everyday life, many quantities are related and change together. Understanding how these
quantities are connected helps learners make sense of the world around them. In mathematics,
these relationships are often studied through proportions and variations, where one quantity
changes depending on another. For example, as children grow older, their height generally
increases, the temperature of water may change with depth, and the length of a shadow changes
depending on the position of the sun during the day. Through exploring related proportions in the
assessment: proportions in the environment, and apply them in real-life situations.
Learning Activity 2: Exploring the concept of Variation.
Exploring the concept of variation enables learners to understand how different quantities change
in relation to each other in real-life situations. Through hands on activities such as investigating
distance and time, number of workers and time taken to complete a task, shadow length and time
